posts.title: The Approval Freshness Rule for Agent Actions
posts.summary: Agent workflows often fail when they act on old approvals in new conditions. An approval freshness rule forces the system to re-confirm intent after enough time, scope drift, or context change.

posts.title: The Observation Window After Agent Policy Changes
posts.summary: Agent policy changes need a period of heightened attention after release. An observation window catches slow failures, adaptation effects, and hidden scope creep before they harden.

posts.title: The Evidence Packet for LLM Release Reviews
posts.summary: A release meeting should not depend on whoever remembers the most context. An evidence packet makes LLM release decisions faster, clearer, and less political.

posts.title: The Approval Surface for Publishing Agents
posts.summary: If a publishing agent only has one approval gate at the end, you may be approving too late. Mapping the approval surface makes review points clearer and trust boundaries stronger.

posts.title: The Quiet-Hours Rule for Autonomous Changes
posts.summary: Autonomous systems should not have identical authority at 2 p.m. and 2 a.m. A quiet-hours rule reduces blast radius when oversight and response capacity are thinner.

posts.title: Release Override Logs Are the Missing Layer in LLM Governance
posts.summary: When teams override LLM release gates without structured evidence, reliability and trust drift over time. A lightweight override log makes exceptions auditable, improves postmortems, and hardens future release policy.

posts.title: The Policy Diff Before You Ship an Agent Change
posts.summary: Agent changes often ship as prompt tweaks or policy edits that are hard to inspect. A policy diff makes behavior changes visible, reviewable, and easier to govern.

posts.title: The Escalation Path for High-Impact Agent Actions
posts.summary: Many agent failures come from doing too much under ambiguity instead of escalating. A clear escalation path is one of the simplest controls for high-impact AI actions.
